topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification flashed across my screen, a jarring intrusion into the quiet hum of my evening. My jaw tightened immediately. It was him. Mark. The same shadow that used to loom over my lunch table, the architect of my early humiliations. A friend request. A deliberate, calculated move designed to pull me back into his orbit. I stared at the profile picture—a forced, overly confident grin—and a slow, cold heat began to spread through my chest. I didn't even bother to open the message. I simply deleted the request, the action crisp and final, a small victory snatched from the jaws of his unwelcome attention.

[story 2]

I picked up my phone, my thumb hovering over the notification, a tremor running through my hand that I quickly suppressed. Seeing his name pop up felt like a physical jolt, unpleasant and sharp. Years melted away, replaced by a sudden, unwelcome recollection of playground taunts and whispered cruelties. A bitter taste coated my tongue. I scrolled through my contacts, my gaze lingering on his name for a fraction of a second too long. A low, almost inaudible scoff escaped my lips. I didn't respond. Instead, I locked the screen, the resulting silence in my room feeling heavy, almost satisfyingly empty.

[story 3]

The alert blinked, demanding attention. It was Mark. My stomach clenched, a tight, unpleasant knot forming just beneath my ribs. I felt a surge of irritation, hot and prickly, like static electricity building before a storm. Why now? After all this time, after I’d built a life separate from his influence, he resurfaced with this digital tether. I leaned back in my chair, crossing my arms tightly over my chest, my expression hardening into something unreadable. I let the notification sit there, a silent challenge. I made no move to engage, letting the sheer audacity of the request hang in the air between us.
